description:
Implement the minimal redesign for `packages/pi-beadwork-extension` so reviewer runs behave like normal exploratory coding agents by default instead of a locked-down strict-JSON-only sandbox.

User-approved scope:
- remove default reviewer isolation flags so pi reviewers keep normal tools/extensions/skills access
- keep validation mandatory before review and preserve coordinator skepticism about reviewer findings
- replace strict-JSON-only prompting with a structured handoff report the coordinator can parse after the review run
- keep verdicts aligned with the requested model (`APPROVE`, `APPROVE WITH NITS`, `REQUEST CHANGES`) while consuming the structured report in the landing flow
- apply the minimal pass plan in `packages/pi-beadwork-extension`: reviewer command, prompt/parser, remediation handling, docs, and tests

Definition of done:
- update reviewer command/prompt/parser flow in `packages/pi-beadwork-extension`
- add/adjust tests covering report parsing and orchestration behavior
- update docs for the new reviewer defaults and handoff format
- run lint, tests, and typecheck successfully for the touched workspace and repo as needed
- commit with an atomic conventional commit before closing the issue

comments.text:
Implementation plan recorded before coding so the redesign survives context loss.

Pass 1 — plumbing / minimal behavior shift
1. Inspect `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/orchestrator.ts` and related tests/docs to locate reviewer command construction, prompt text, parsing, and remediation flow.
2. Remove the default `--no-tools --no-extensions --no-skills` reviewer isolation from pi reviewer command generation so reviewers behave like normal exploratory agents by default.
3. Replace strict-JSON-only reviewer prompting/parsing with a structured handoff format that still yields machine-readable verdict/findings for the coordinator.
4. Keep validation as a hard precondition before review; do not weaken existing lint/test/typecheck enforcement.

Pass 2 — coordinator/tests/docs
5. Update coordinator consumption/remediation logic so structured reviewer findings are parsed, scoped, and handled without trusting the reviewer blindly.
6. Add/update tests for command generation, reviewer report parsing, and verdict/remediation behavior.
7. Update package docs to describe the new reviewer defaults and handoff expectations.
8. Run lint/test/typecheck, fix fallout, and commit atomically with a conventional commit.

Goal: minimal code-change redesign, no extra restriction-config surface unless implementation proves it is truly necessary.

title: Revise pi-beadwork reviewer to use exploratory agent reviews with structured handoff
